properties.remark: UP TO FOUR INCHES OF WATER ON THE ROAD AT ASHMENT AND 17TH STREETS IN IDAHO FALLS.
WIND GUST AT AIRPORT PRIOR TO VERY HEAVY RAINFALL.
6 TO 9 INCH TREE LIMB BLOWN DOWN OFF BY SCHOOL AND A 3 INCH LIMB WAS BLOWN ACROSS ROAD. WINDS ESTIMATED AT OVER 50 MPH. MEASURED RAINFALL OF 0.10 INCH FROM EDGE OF PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
10 DIAMETER BY 15FT LONG COTTONWOOD BRANCH WAS BLOWN DOWN.
FALLEN BRANCHES FROM TREES. ONE TRE AS FALLEN ON SERVICE LINE. PRECIPITATION OF 0.27 INCHES IN LESS THAN 15 MINUTES.
LIMBS DOWN ALONG HWY 33
NEAR LESLIE...IDAHO.
GUSTS ESTIMATED. VISIBILITY ON HWY 20 FROM INL TO ARCO REDUCED TO 5 FEET DUE TO BLOWING DUST. HIGHWAY PATROL ON HIGWAY CONTROLLING TRAFFIC.
50MPH WINDS ALONG WITH 8IN BY 8FT BRANCH BLOWN DOWN.

SUBMITTED TO IDAHO STATE JOURNAL VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. REPORTED SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN AS WELL AS SOME POWERLINES AND ROAD SIGNS. TIME BASED FROM RADAR.
POWER WAS OUT AS OF 4PM. PART OF LARGER POWER OUTAGES IN BLACKFOOT AND NEARBY AREAS. 1350 PEOPLE AFFECTED AT ONE POINT.
MEASURED HAIL STONE IN CHUBBUCK.
50 TO 60 MPH WINDS REMOVED LIMBS FROM TREES AND SIDING BLOWN OFF OF HOUSES IN MOORELAND.
40 PLUS MPH WIND GUSTS. THREE INCH TREE LIMBS ARE BROKEN FROM TREES. VINYL FENCE HAS DAMAGE DUE TO BROKEN SLATS TORN FREE OF POSTS.
SPOTTER RETURNED TODAY TO FIND SOME SHINGLES RIPPED OFF HOME, A FLIPPED FUEL TANK, SEVERAL DOWNED TREES AND A MAILBOX THAT WAS RIPPED OUT. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
WESTON AND PRESTON EXPERIENCED 45 TO 50 MPH WIND GUSTS. SEVERAL POWER POLES ARE DOWN. NO HAIL REPORTED.
REPORTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA TO CHANNEL 3. HAIL STONES OF VARIOUS SIZES COVERING THE GROUND. TIME BASED ON RADAR.
1FT DIAMETER TREE BROKEN AS THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH. THIS IS A CORRECTION FROM JUNE 1ST EVENT.
MEASURED WIND GUST OF 76 MPH. GUST SNAPPED OFF QUAKING ASPEN TREE.
REPORTED THAT A NEIGHBOR'S BATHROOM WINDOW WAS BLOWN OUT. BASED ON OTHER REPORT AND RADAR.
POWER POLES ARE DOWN. 4-6 INCH TREES ARE DOWN. POWER LINES OVER HOUSES AND ROADS. EFFORTS UNDERWAY TO CLOSE ROADS UNTIL POWER LINES ARE REMOVED.
1FT DIAMETER TREE WAS BROKEN AS THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH.

HAIL BEGAN AS DIME-SIZE. RAIN AND HAIL MIX SO HEAVY, VISIBILITY IS LESS THAN 15 FEET. WITH EASING OF RAIN, ESTIMATE THREE INCHES OF STANDING WATER LIES BETWEEN HOUSE AND BARN.

ESTIMATED GUST TO 75 MPH OCCURRED AS THE THUNDERSTORM CORE PASSED OVER THE CITY PRODUCING THE SHORT-LIVED, VIOLENT, WIND GUST. LARGE DIAMETER TREE DAMAGED A TRUCK, TRACTOR, AND HOME. TIME OF OCCURRENCE, ESTIMATED BY RADAR FROM 717 TO 723 PM.

TRAINED SPOTTER TRAVELING ON THE SALEM HIGHWAY 4 MILES NORTH OF REXBURG, REPORTED SEVERAL 3-4 INCH TREE BRANCHES LAYING ON THE ROAD. ESTIMATED WINDS 50 TO 60 MPH WITH TREES SWAYING STRONGLY.

SPOTTER ALSO OBSERVED A FEW HAILSTONES LARGER THAN ONE INCH. GUSTY WINDS ALSO OBSERVED WITH NO GOOD ESTIMATE OF WIND VELOCITY. NO WIND DAMAGE OBSERVED.
